questions.question: A doctor who specializes in diseases of the nose
questions.explanation: Rhinologist - A Rhinologist is a specialist doctor who focuses on the medical and surgical treatment of conditions affecting the nose and sinuses. Otologist :- A doctor who specializes in diseases of the ear. Pathologist :- A doctor who is an expert in pathology, and examines dead bodies to find out why a person has died.चिकित्सक Podiatrist :- A person who treats the feet and their ailments; a chiropodist.

questions.question: Identify the grammatical error in the sentence below. If you had spoken to my mother, she would tell you where I was.
questions.explanation: B) ‘tell you’ के बदले ‘have told you’ का प्रयोग होगा क्योंकि यह Third Conditional Sentence है। If + Past Perfect (had spoken) के साथ Result Clause में would have + Past Participle आता है; जैसे— If you had worked hard, you would have succeeded. • ‘have told you’ will be used instead of ‘tell you’ because the sentence follows the third conditional pattern (If + Past Perfect → would have + V³); Like— If she had called me, I would have helped her.

questions.question: Pick the correctly used homonym.
options: She was born in the barn.
She was borne in the barn.
The weight was barn by him.
questions.explanation: A) She was born in the barn. यहाँ born का अर्थ है — जन्म लिया हुआ। विकल्प 1 में born का प्रयोग सही है। borne का अर्थ होता है — उठाया/सहन किया हुआ, जो जन्म के लिए प्रयोग नहीं होता। बाकी वाक्यों में born / barn का प्रयोग गलत है। born is correctly used to mean came into life. Sentence 1 uses born properly. borne means carried or endured, not given birth to. The other sentences contain incorrect usage — hence the first sentence is correct.

questions.question: What is the antonym of "Parsimonious"?
questions.explanation: C) Parsimonious (adjective) – extremely unwilling to spend money; stingy, miserly कंजूस / मितव्ययी Antonym: Generous (adjective) – willing to give freely; liberal उदार • Stingy (adjective) – unwilling to give or spend कंजूस • Frugal (adjective) – careful and economical in spending मितव्ययी • Economical (adjective) – using resources carefully किफायती

questions.question: What is the antonym of "Somnolent"?
questions.explanation: A) Somnolent (adjective) – sleepy, drowsy, inclined to sleep उनींदा / आलसी Antonym: Awake (adjective) – alert, not sleeping, fully conscious जागरूक / सतर्क • Dull (adjective) – lacking interest or alertness नीरस • Lethargic (adjective) – sluggish, lacking energy सुस्त • Sluggish (adjective) – slow-moving, inactive मंद

questions.question: Rearrange the following sentence parts to form a meaningful and grammatically correct paragraph: P. The old man, with his kind eyes and twisted hands, smiled softly. Q. He had lived a whole life as a woodcarver, and his store was a museum of fragile works of art. R. He held a tiny bird he was carving, his thumb caressing the slippery wood. S. They all had a story, a memory, a piece of his soul.
questions.explanation: A) P Q R S The old man, with his kind eyes and twisted hands, smiled softly. He had lived a whole life as a woodcarver, and his store was a museum of fragile works of art. He held a tiny bird he was carving, his thumb caressing the slippery wood. They all had a story, a memory, a piece of his soul. Sentence P सबसे पहले आता है क्योंकि यह paragraph का introduction है और main character को introduce करता है: “The old man, with his kind eyes and twisted hands, smiled softly.” यहाँ “The old man” subject है और “smiled” verb है। यह paragraph की शुरुआत set करता है और reader को scene दिखाता है। Sentence Q, P के बाद आता है क्योंकि यह old man के background और context को explain करता है: उसने पूरा जीवन woodcarver के रूप में बिताया है, और उसकी store एक museum जैसी है। यहाँ “He” subject है और “had lived… was” verbs हैं। यह P में introduced character के बारे में additional detail देता है। Sentence R, Q के बाद आता है क्योंकि अब paragraph में action दिखाई देता है: वह tiny bird carve कर रहा है, और उसके हाथ में skill और delicacy दिखाई देती है। यहाँ “He” subject है और “held… caressing” verbs हैं। यह Q में बताया गया background और skill को show करता है। Sentence S paragraph का last sentence है क्योंकि यह old man की emotional depth और connection को conclude करता है: सभी objects में उसकी कहानी, memory और soul का हिस्सा है। यहाँ “They” subject है और “had” verb है। यह natural conclusion देता है और paragraph को wrap up करता है। Sentence P comes first because it introduces the paragraph and the main character: “The old man, with his kind eyes and twisted hands, smiled softly.” Here, “The old man” is the subject and “smiled” is the verb. It sets the beginning of the paragraph and gives the reader the initial scene. Sentence Q comes after P because it explains the old man’s background and context: he had lived a whole life as a woodcarver, and his store was like a museum. Here, “He” is the subject and “had lived… was” are the verbs. It provides additional details about the character introduced in P. Sentence R comes after Q because the paragraph now shows action: he is holding a tiny bird he is carving, demonstrating his skill and delicacy. Here, “He” is the subject and “held… caressing” are the verbs. This shows the practical aspect of the skill described in Q. Sentence S comes last because it concludes the paragraph by highlighting the old man’s emotional depth and connection: all the objects carry a story, a memory, and a piece of his soul. Here, “They” is the subject and “had” is the verb. It gives a natural conclusion and wraps up the paragraph.

questions.question: Identify the grammatical error in the sentence below. Amazon Prime Day is going to kick up the holiday season and holiday shopping for many consumers.
questions.explanation: A) ‘going to kick up’ के बदले ‘going to kick off’ का प्रयोग होगा क्योंकि kick off का अर्थ किसी चीज़ की शुरुआत करना होता है, जबकि kick up का अर्थ हंगामा/उथल-पुथल पैदा करना होता है, जो यहाँ संदर्भ के अनुसार गलत है। • ‘kick off’ is correct, not ‘kick up’, since the sentence talks about the beginning of the holiday season and shopping, not creating disturbance.

questions.question: Choose the correctly spelt word:
questions.explanation: A) The correct spelling is ‘Cigarette’, which means a small roll of finely cut tobacco wrapped in paper for smoking — सिगरेट.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the word: Turgid
questions.explanation: B) Turgid (adjective) – bombastic, swollen, inflated in style or language आडंबरपूर्ण / फूला हुआ Synonym: Inflated (adjective) – exaggerated, overblown, puffed up बढ़ा-चढ़ाकर • Concise (adjective) – brief and to the point संक्षिप्त • Simple (adjective) – easy, plain, not complicated सरल • Plain (adjective) – clear, straightforward, unadorned साधारण

questions.question: Select the alternative which best expresses the meaning of idioms/phrases. At sixes and sevens
options: If someone is anxious before sitting an examination
If someone is in a state of confusion
questions.explanation: C) At sixes and sevens (idiom) – If someone is in a state of confusion अव्यवस्था या उलझन की स्थिति में होना

questions.question: Read the passage and answer the following question. Despite significant progress in mental health awareness, visiting a psychiatrist remains a taboo for many people in India. Deep rooted cultural beliefs, lack of education about mental illnesses, and the fear of social judgment often prevent individuals from seeking professional help when they need it most. Instead, many people choose to suffer in silence or rely solely on family advice, which may not always be sufficient. However, mental health experts emphasize that consulting a qualified psychiatrist is not a sign of weakness but a step towards healing and self care. Just as one would visit a doctor for physical ailments, mental health conditions deserve equal attention and professional treatment. Unfortunately, the stigma attached to mental health persists, discouraging open conversations and timely intervention. Breaking this taboo requires collective effort – families, schools, workplaces, and communities must foster an environment where seeking mental health support is normalized and encouraged. Only then can India hope to reduce the burden of untreated mental illnesses and improve overall societal well-being. Identify the tense used in the sentence: "Just as one would visit a doctor for physical ailments; mental health conditions deserve equal attention."
questions.explanation: C) ‘Present simple’ का प्रयोग होगा क्योंकि sentence एक general truth और universal principle को व्यक्त कर रहा है। यहाँ “mental health conditions deserve equal attention” किसी विशेष समय की घटना नहीं बल्कि सामान्य सत्य को बताता है। इसलिए simple present tense सबसे उपयुक्त है। • ‘Present simple’ should be used because the sentence expresses a general truth and a universal principle. Here, “mental health conditions deserve equal attention” is not tied to any specific time but states a general fact. Thus, simple present tense is the most appropriate choice.

questions.question: Read the passage and answer the following question. Despite significant progress in mental health awareness, visiting a psychiatrist remains a taboo for many people in India. Deep rooted cultural beliefs, lack of education about mental illnesses, and the fear of social judgment often prevent individuals from seeking professional help when they need it most. Instead, many people choose to suffer in silence or rely solely on family advice, which may not always be sufficient. However, mental health experts emphasize that consulting a qualified psychiatrist is not a sign of weakness but a step towards healing and self care. Just as one would visit a doctor for physical ailments, mental health conditions deserve equal attention and professional treatment. Unfortunately, the stigma attached to mental health persists, discouraging open conversations and timely intervention. Breaking this taboo requires collective effort – families, schools, workplaces, and communities must foster an environment where seeking mental health support is normalized and encouraged. Only then can India hope to reduce the burden of untreated mental illnesses and improve overall societal well-being. Identify the voice used in the sentence: "Instead, many people choose to suffer in silence."
questions.explanation: B) ‘Active voice’ का प्रयोग होगा क्योंकि sentence में subject “many people” स्वयं action कर रहा है — “choose to suffer in silence.” यहाँ क्रिया सीधे subject द्वारा की जा रही है, न कि subject पर की जा रही है। इसलिए यह active voice है। • ‘Active voice’ should be used because in the sentence the subject “many people” is directly performing the action — “choose to suffer in silence.” The action is done by the subject, not received by it. Thus, it is active voice.

questions.question: Read the passage and answer the following question. Despite significant progress in mental health awareness, visiting a psychiatrist remains a taboo for many people in India. Deep rooted cultural beliefs, lack of education about mental illnesses, and the fear of social judgment often prevent individuals from seeking professional help when they need it most. Instead, many people choose to suffer in silence or rely solely on family advice, which may not always be sufficient. However, mental health experts emphasize that consulting a qualified psychiatrist is not a sign of weakness but a step towards healing and self care. Just as one would visit a doctor for physical ailments, mental health conditions deserve equal attention and professional treatment. Unfortunately, the stigma attached to mental health persists, discouraging open conversations and timely intervention. Breaking this taboo requires collective effort – families, schools, workplaces, and communities must foster an environment where seeking mental health support is normalized and encouraged. Only then can India hope to reduce the burden of untreated mental illnesses and improve overall societal well-being. What does the word "taboo" most likely mean in the passage?
options: A forbidden or socially unacceptable practice
questions.explanation: C) ‘A forbidden or socially unacceptable practice’ का प्रयोग होगा क्योंकि passage में “taboo” उस चीज़ को दर्शा रहा है जिसे समाज में खुलकर स्वीकार नहीं किया जाता। यहाँ psychiatrist के पास जाना लोगों द्वारा social judgment के डर से टाला जाता है, इसलिए यह socially unacceptable practice के अर्थ में प्रयोग हुआ है। • ‘A forbidden or socially unacceptable practice’ should be used because in the passage “taboo” refers to something society does not openly accept. Here, visiting a psychiatrist is avoided due to fear of social judgment, so the word clearly means a socially unacceptable practice. Thus, option C is the most appropriate choice.

questions.question: Read the passage and answer the following question. Despite significant progress in mental health awareness, visiting a psychiatrist remains a taboo for many people in India. Deep rooted cultural beliefs, lack of education about mental illnesses, and the fear of social judgment often prevent individuals from seeking professional help when they need it most. Instead, many people choose to suffer in silence or rely solely on family advice, which may not always be sufficient. However, mental health experts emphasize that consulting a qualified psychiatrist is not a sign of weakness but a step towards healing and self care. Just as one would visit a doctor for physical ailments, mental health conditions deserve equal attention and professional treatment. Unfortunately, the stigma attached to mental health persists, discouraging open conversations and timely intervention. Breaking this taboo requires collective effort – families, schools, workplaces, and communities must foster an environment where seeking mental health support is normalized and encouraged. Only then can India hope to reduce the burden of untreated mental illnesses and improve overall societal well-being. What does the phrase "suffer in silence" mean in the context of the passage?
options: To speak openly about one's problems
To get treatment without anyone knowing
To discuss mental health with family only
To hide one's pain and not seek help
questions.explanation: D) Suffer in silence – To hide one's pain and not seek help (अपना दुख छिपाकर सहना और मदद न लेना)

questions.question: Read the passage and answer the following question. Despite significant progress in mental health awareness, visiting a psychiatrist remains a taboo for many people in India. Deep rooted cultural beliefs, lack of education about mental illnesses, and the fear of social judgment often prevent individuals from seeking professional help when they need it most. Instead, many people choose to suffer in silence or rely solely on family advice, which may not always be sufficient. However, mental health experts emphasize that consulting a qualified psychiatrist is not a sign of weakness but a step towards healing and self care. Just as one would visit a doctor for physical ailments, mental health conditions deserve equal attention and professional treatment. Unfortunately, the stigma attached to mental health persists, discouraging open conversations and timely intervention. Breaking this taboo requires collective effort – families, schools, workplaces, and communities must foster an environment where seeking mental health support is normalized and encouraged. Only then can India hope to reduce the burden of untreated mental illnesses and improve overall societal well-being. Why do many people in India avoid visiting a psychiatrist?
options: Cultural beliefs and fear of social judgment
questions.explanation: C) ‘Cultural beliefs and fear of social judgment’ का प्रयोग होगा क्योंकि passage स्पष्ट रूप से बताता है कि deep rooted cultural beliefs, mental illness के बारे में जागरूकता की कमी, और social judgment का डर लोगों को psychiatrist के पास जाने से रोकता है। इसलिए यही सबसे उपयुक्त कारण है। • ‘Cultural beliefs and fear of social judgment’ should be used because the passage clearly states that deep-rooted cultural beliefs, lack of awareness, and fear of social judgment prevent people from visiting a psychiatrist. Thus, option C is the most appropriate choice.

questions.question: One of these words is spelled incorrectly. Which one is it?
questions.explanation: B) The word ‘Conscientous’ is spelled incorrectly. The correct spelling is ‘Conscientious’, which means careful, diligent, and guided by a sense of duty — कर्तव्यनिष्ठ, ईमानदार, सजग.

questions.question: One who has exaggerated anxiety about one's health
questions.explanation: Hypochondriac :- A person who is always worried about his/her health and believes he/she is ill, even when there is nothing wrong. Pediatric :- Relating to the medical care of children. बाल चिकित्सा Verbatim :- Exactly as it was spoken or written. शब्‍दशः Petrify :- To frighten someone a lot, especially so that they are unable to move or speak. पत्थर बनाना, कड़ा बनाना

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the word: TRANSHISTORICAL
questions.explanation: A) Transhistorical (adjective) – Existing beyond or outside history; not limited to one historical period इतिहास से परे / समय से स्वतंत्र Synonym: Timeless (adjective) – Eternal, everlasting, enduring, ageless, perpetual कालातीत, समय से परे • Ancient (adjective) – Very old, from distant past प्राचीन • Recent (adjective) – Newly occurred, modern हालिया / हाल का • Futuristic (adjective) – Ahead of its time, related to the future भविष्यवादी

questions.question: Change the following sentence into the Active form. A new computer has been bought for the office.
options: The office bought a new computer.
Someone has bought a new computer for the office
A new computer was bought for the office
Someone is buying a new computer for the office.
questions.explanation: B) Someone has bought a new computer for the office. Passive से Active Voice में बदलते समय Passive वाक्य का Subject, Active वाक्य का Object बन जाता है और यदि agent (by whom) नहीं दिया हो, तो Someone जैसे सामान्य Subject का प्रयोग किया जाता है। Verb को Passive से Active में बदलते समय वही Tense रखा जाता है। दिए गए वाक्य में “A new computer” Subject है, “has been bought” Verb (Present Perfect Passive) है और “for the office” Object/Prepositional phrase है। Active में बदलने पर “Someone” Subject बनता है, Verb “has bought” (Present Perfect Active) का प्रयोग होता है और “a new computer” Object बन जाता है। To change a sentence from Passive to Active Voice, the object of the passive sentence becomes the object in the active sentence, and if the agent is not mentioned, “Someone” is used as the subject. The tense remains the same. Here, “A new computer” is the subject, “has been bought” is the verb in Present Perfect Passive, and “for the office” is the object phrase. In the active form, “Someone” becomes the subject, the verb changes to “has bought” (Present Perfect Active), and “a new computer” becomes the object.

questions.question: Fill in the blank with the most appropriate phrase: The board's decision to withdraw the bid came completely ____ for the stakeholders.
questions.explanation: B) out of the blue (phrase) – Unexpectedly; without any warning अचानक/बिना पूर्व संकेत के • off the wall (phrase) – Unusual or eccentric अजीब/अनोखा • behind closed doors (phrase) – In private; not publicly गुप्त रूप से • under fire (phrase) – Being criticized आलोचना के घेरे में

questions.question: Which of the following options is NOT one of the meanings of the homonym 'condition'?
options: To improve the hardness and elasticity of things
The physical state of something or someone
To train someone to behave in a certain way
A requirement that must be met for something to happen
questions.explanation: A) To improve the hardness and elasticity of things condition के अर्थ होते हैं — किसी वस्तु/व्यक्ति की अवस्था, किसी को किसी व्यवहार के लिए प्रशिक्षित करना, और कोई शर्त जो पूरी होनी आवश्यक हो। लेकिन किसी चीज़ की कठोरता या लचीलापन बढ़ाना condition का अर्थ नहीं है। इसलिए यह गलत विकल्प है। The word condition can mean the state of something, to train someone to behave in a certain way, or a requirement that must be met. It does not mean to improve hardness and elasticity — hence option A is correct.

questions.question: Choose the correct meaning of the idiom: Are on a collision course
options: Moving towards each other in a peaceful way
Having different goals but no conflict
questions.explanation: A) Are on a collision course (idiom) – Heading towards an inevitable conflict टकराव की ओर बढ़ना, जहाँ संघर्ष होना तय है

questions.question: Choose the correct passive voice transformation of the sentence: The dedicated students, who had been preparing all night, were answering the difficult questions during the final examination.
options: The difficult questions were answered by the dedicated students during the final examination.
The difficult questions were being answered by the dedicated students during the final examination.
The difficult questions had been answered by the dedicated students during the final examination.
The difficult questions are being answered by the dedicated students during the final examination.
questions.explanation: B) The difficult questions were being answered by the dedicated students during the final examination. Active से Passive Voice में बदलते समय Active वाक्य का Object, Passive वाक्य का Subject बन जाता है और Verb को उसी Tense के Passive रूप में बदला जाता है। दिए गए वाक्य में "The dedicated students" Subject है, "were answering" Verb (Past Continuous) है और "the difficult questions" Object है। Passive में बदलने पर Object "The difficult questions" Subject बन गया है, Verb "were answering" को "were being answered" (Past Continuous Passive) में बदला गया है और "by the dedicated students" जोड़ा गया है। समय-सूचक वाक्यांश "during the final examination" बना रहता है। To change a sentence from Active to Passive Voice, the object of the active sentence becomes the subject of the passive sentence, and the verb is changed into its correct passive form according to the tense. Here, "The dedicated students" is the subject, "were answering" is the verb in Past Continuous, and "the difficult questions" is the object. In the passive form, "The difficult questions" becomes the subject, the verb changes to "were being answered" (Past Continuous Passive), and the agent is added using by. The time expression remains unchanged.

questions.question: Choose the best option to replace the underlined part to improve the given sentence. If no substitution is required, select 'No substitution.' Everyone clapped as she stepped from the car.
questions.explanation: C) 'stepped from the car' के बदले 'stepped out of the car' का प्रयोग होगा क्योंकि वाहन से बाहर आने के लिए सही idiomatic expression step out of होता है। • 'stepped out of' will be used instead of 'stepped from' because the correct phrasal usage for leaving a vehicle is step out of; Like— He stepped out of the taxi.

questions.question: Fill in the blank with the most appropriate preposition: The athlete was disqualified ______ violating the rules of the competition.
questions.explanation: B) ‘for’ का प्रयोग होगा क्योंकि अंग्रेज़ी में “disqualified for” एक fixed expression है, जिसका अर्थ है किसी गलती या नियम तोड़ने के कारण अयोग्य घोषित किया जाना। Sentence में “violating the rules” कारण (reason) बता रहा है, इसलिए “for” सबसे उपयुक्त preposition है। • ‘For’ should be used because “disqualified for” is a standard collocation in English, used when someone is declared ineligible due to a specific reason or wrongdoing. Since “violating the rules” states the cause, “for” is the most appropriate preposition.
